sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Apple Races to Tweak Software Ahead of Looming US Watch Ban
feed.summary: Apple Inc. is rapidly working on software adjustments to its Apple Watch series to circumvent a looming U.S. ban. The changes primarily target the blood oxygen level measurement feature, which a company called Masimo Corp. claims infringes on its patents. These modifications include altering algorithms for oxygen saturation detection and data presentation to consumers.

feed.title: Qorvo to sell Chinese assembly, test factories to Luxshare ...
feed.summary: Qorvo Inc. has agreed to sell its assembly and test facilities in Beijing and Dezhou, China, to Luxshare Precision Industry Co. Ltd. The deal includes Luxshare acquiring the facilities' assets, operations, and workforce, while Qorvo will continue to be supplied through a long-term agreement. This move allows Qorvo to streamline its manufacturing network while Luxshare expands its role in the electronics manufacturing services sector, particularly within the Apple supply chain.

feed.title: Apple terminates credit-card and savings partnership with Goldman Sachs
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has initiated the termination of its credit card and savings account partnership with Goldman Sachs Group Inc., proposing to end the collaboration within 12 to 15 months. This move follows Goldman Sachs' struggles with its consumer banking division, which has incurred losses despite the strong performance of Apple Card and Apple Savings. Apple is now seeking a new banking provider, with JPMorgan Chase & Co. and American Express Company emerging as top contenders.

feed.title: Why Apple Had to Disable the Blood-Oxygen Sensor in Its Watches
feed.summary: Apple has begun selling new versions of its latest Apple Watch models with the blood oxygen sensing feature disabled. This action stems from a patent dispute with Masimo Corp., a medical technology company, which successfully argued that Apple violated its patents. A US International Trade Commission ruling and subsequent appeals court decision led Apple to remove the feature rather than face a sales ban on its Watch Series 9 and Ultra 2 models.

feed.title: Meta: Sell it? Buy it?
feed.summary: Meta's stock price has experienced significant volatility in recent years, oscillating from a high of $384 in August 2021 to below $100 in October 2022, before rebounding to around $340. This article explores the company's journey from being considered a "sell" to a "buy," focusing on challenges such as Apple's privacy policy changes and competition from TikTok, and how Meta has responded by investing heavily in AI and re-evaluating its metaverse strategy. The future success of Meta is tied to its AI applications across its businesses, especially in advertising and new ventures.

feed.title: “Defy Dementia with Sound.” Pixie Dust Technologies, Shionogi and Shionogi Healthcare create Gamma Wave Sound to boost everyday cognitive care.
feed.summary: Pixie Dust Technologies, Shionogi, and Shionogi Healthcare have launched "Memory" and "Tomorrow," two audio experiences utilizing 40Hz gamma wave technology to support cognitive function in dementia patients. This initiative, part of the Dementia Care Project by Gamma Wave Sound, aims to provide non-invasive cognitive stimulation through sound, with ongoing studies indicating that gamma waves can promote brain function and reduce atrophy. The project is expanding globally with collaborations across various industries and music artists.

feed.title: U.S. Fitness Tracker Market Size, Growth and Trends [2030]
feed.summary: The U.S. fitness tracker market was valued at USD 13.69 billion in 2022 and is projected to reach a significant size by 2030, growing at a CAGR of 18.9% during the forecast period. This growth is driven by the increasing prevalence of chronic diseases, high adoption of fitness monitoring devices, and technological advancements like integration with smartphones and advanced features. Despite potential inaccuracies, the market sees strong demand, particularly for smartwatches, with key players continuously innovating to meet consumer needs.

feed.title: Why These 3 Semiconductor Stocks Should Be on Your Radar in 2024
feed.summary: The article highlights three semiconductor stocks—Qualcomm (QCOM), United Microelectronics Corporation (UMC), and Navitas Semiconductor (NVTS)—that are poised for growth in 2024, driven by advancements in AI, electric vehicles, and 5G technology. It evaluates each company's market position, financial health, and growth catalysts, emphasizing their undervaluation and innovative contributions to the industry. The piece suggests these stocks offer high risk-reward opportunities for investors looking to capitalize on the expanding semiconductor market.

feed.title: Intellexa and Cytrox: From fixer-upper to Intel Agency-grade spyware
feed.summary: Cisco Talos has conducted an in-depth analysis of Intellexa, a spyware vendor previously known as Cytrox, detailing its operational paradigms, procedures, and the evolution of its Predator spyware. The analysis reveals that Intellexa's Predator spyware continues to evade detection, offers persistence even after device reboots, and demonstrates a sophisticated business model with high-priced solutions targeting state-sponsored agencies. Despite public disclosures and the US adding Intellexa to its Entity List, the company effectively continues its operations by adapting to patched exploits and maintaining plausible deniability through its sales and delivery methods.

feed.title: QARP ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:QARP
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the Xtrackers Russell 1000 US Quality at a Reasonable Price ETF (QARP), detailing its investment strategy, holdings, and performance. QARP focuses on US large-cap companies with a blend of quality and value metrics, giving twice as much weight to quality. The ETF has an expense ratio of 0.19% and aims to provide exposure to the Russell 1000 Slowdown Factor Index.

feed.title: PLDT warns public on AI-generated scams
feed.summary: PLDT and Smart Communications are warning the public about the rise of AI-generated scams, which are becoming increasingly sophisticated and convincing. Criminals are leveraging AI to create polished messages and visuals that mimic official communications, making it harder for individuals to distinguish genuine messages from fraudulent ones. PLDT advises checking the source of emails and texts and adhering to the basic rule of not communicating with strangers to avoid falling victim to these scams.

feed.title: Apple files appeal after Biden administration allows US ban on watch imports
feed.summary: Apple has appealed a U.S. import ban on its watches, which stems from a patent dispute with medical monitoring company Masimo. The ban, allowed by the Biden administration, targets Apple Watches with blood-oxygen level features. Apple has also requested an emergency halt to the ban while awaiting a decision from U.S. Customs and Border Protection on redesigned versions of its watches.

feed.title: T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF): A Promising Active Fund
feed.summary: The T. Rowe Price Capital Appreciation Equity ETF (TCAF) has shown significant growth since its launch in June 2023, accumulating over $600 million in assets. The fund focuses on high-quality U.S. large-cap companies, employing a bottom-up stock selection approach with top holdings like Apple, Microsoft, and Amazon. TCAF's investment philosophy emphasizes strong performance in areas like technology, healthcare, and consumer discretionary.

feed.title: How I used the Garmin epix Pro to train for Spartan Race's Trifecta Weekend
feed.summary: The author details their experience using the Garmin epix Pro to train for and complete Spartan Race's Trifecta Weekend, which involves finishing three races (Sprint, Super, Beast) in one weekend. The article highlights how the smartwatch's features like training readiness, body battery, heart rate monitoring, and race prediction were crucial for their rigorous training regimen and successful completion of the demanding events. The author expresses that the Garmin epix Pro not only enhanced their athletic performance but also motivated them toward healthier habits and served as a worthy investment in their fitness journey.

feed.title: Here Are All 49 Stocks Warren Buffett Holds for Berkshire Hathaway's $371 Billion Portfolio
feed.summary: This article details the 49 stocks held in Warren Buffett's Berkshire Hathaway portfolio, valued at $371 billion. It highlights that over 85% of the assets are concentrated in eight major holdings like Apple and Bank of America, while also listing other billion-dollar investments and smaller holdings attributed to Buffett's lieutenants. The piece emphasizes Buffett's preference for financial and cyclical businesses, his valuation-driven approach, and recent investments in homebuilders.

feed.title: Apple's stock underperformed top tech peers in 2023 due to longest revenue slide in 22 years
feed.summary: Apple's stock underperformed its mega-cap tech peers in 2023, experiencing its longest revenue slide in 22 years with four consecutive quarters of declining sales. This decline was attributed to a combination of a weak economic environment for smartphones and PCs, and company-specific issues such as the lack of new iPad models and an Apple Watch sales ban. Despite a 49% stock rally for the year, Apple lagged behind competitors like Nvidia, Meta, Tesla, Amazon, Alphabet, and Microsoft.

feed.title: What to expect from Sonos in 2024 – headphones, new Arc and a big surprise
feed.summary: In 2024, Sonos is set to expand its product line significantly, venturing into new categories like premium over-ear headphones and potentially a streaming video box. The company also plans major upgrades to its existing portfolio, including an advanced voice control system (Voice 2.0), a second-generation Roam, and a new flagship Sonos Arc soundbar. These moves follow a challenging financial year for Sonos, indicating a strategy focused on growth and diversification.

feed.title: Cyber Hornet S&P 500 and Bitcoin 75/25 Strategy ETF ETF Price, Holdings, & News (NASDAQ:ZZZ)
feed.summary: The Cyber Hornet S&P 500 and Bitcoin 75/25 Strategy ETF (ZZZ) is an exchange-traded fund launched on December 28, 2023, by Onefund, which invests in a 75/25 blend of US large-cap stocks and front-month CME Bitcoin futures. It does not directly invest in Bitcoin's spot price. The ETF has an aggregate rating of "Moderate Buy," assets under management of $6.02 million, and a net expense ratio of 1.01%.

feed.title: Apple Inc bets big on India as it opens first flagship store
feed.summary: Apple Inc. has opened its first flagship store in Mumbai, India, marking a significant expansion into the world's second-largest smartphone market. CEO Tim Cook was present for the launch, emphasizing the company's commitment to India as both a growing market and a potential manufacturing hub. This move aligns with India's government efforts to boost local manufacturing, with Apple aiming for 25% of its global production to come from India within five years.

feed.title: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing to delay chip production in Phoenix for a year. Here's why
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing (TSMC) announced a one-year delay in the start of chip production at its Phoenix facility, pushing the timeline to 2025 due to a lack of skilled employees for specialized equipment installation. The company also anticipates higher production costs in Phoenix compared to Taiwan and is sending more specialized workers to address the issues. This delay comes as TSMC reported its first quarterly profit decline in nearly four years amid lower demand for consumer electronics.

feed.title: IYW: Bill Gates Bought This Tech ETF. Should You?
feed.summary: This article examines Bill Gates' recent acquisition of the iShares U.S. Technology ETF (IYW) in the third quarter, as revealed by 13F filings. It highlights IYW's strong long-term performance, comprehensive portfolio of top U.S. tech companies, and analyst ratings, while also noting its expense ratio compared to peers. The piece suggests that Gates' investment indicates continued opportunity in the tech sector.

feed.title: Qualcomm, Iridium Take Aim at Satellite-to-Mobile Phone Market
feed.summary: Qualcomm and Iridium Communications are collaborating to introduce technology enabling satellite messaging for smartphone users in areas without cellular service. This partnership will initially offer a satellite-based emergency service for Android devices equipped with Qualcomm's premium chips. The move aims to bring satellite connectivity to a broader market, similar to the feature available on newer iPhones.

feed.title: The Cheap Trick Alphabet Is Using To Boost Its Share Price
feed.summary: Alphabet's 20-for-1 stock split, following similar moves by Apple and Tesla, is unlikely to fundamentally change the company's valuation but aims to make shares more accessible to smaller investors and potentially gain entry into the Dow Jones Industrial Average. While some tech giants saw significant share price boosts post-split, Alphabet's future success might be tempered by changing market conditions. The article suggests Amazon could be the next tech giant to undertake a similar split to boost its stock.

feed.title: How Much Longer Can US Stocks Keep Beating The World?
feed.summary: US stocks have outperformed global markets for nearly 14 years, a period attributed to tech giants and resulting in higher valuations compared to other developed economies. The article questions the sustainability of this dominance and suggests ETFs for investors who anticipate a shift, allowing exposure to non-US developed markets.

feed.title: Sophia Velastegui
feed.summary: Sophia Velastegui is Senior Vice President and Chief Product Officer of Aptiv, an automotive and autonomous tech company, where she drives innovation and growth. She previously held significant roles at Microsoft, Google/Alphabet, Apple, and Applied Materials, and is recognized for her board directorships and contributions to AI and emerging technologies. Velastegui is an award-winning engineer with degrees from Georgia Tech and UC Berkeley, and has furthered her corporate governance expertise at Stanford Law and Harvard Business School.

feed.title: Apple Pay
feed.summary: This article from Barclays Private Bank details how to use Apple Pay with Barclays debit cards. It covers the benefits of Apple Pay such as fast, secure, and contactless payments for any amount, and provides instructions for adding cards, setting up, paying in-store, online, and in-app, as well as managing transactions and cards. Eligibility requirements for using Apple Pay with Barclays are also outlined.

feed.title: The Financial Success of Apple Inc.
feed.summary: This article examines the financial success of Apple Inc., focusing on key financial indicators such as net income, operating cash flow, free cash flow, and capital structure. It highlights Apple's consistent growth in profitability and operational efficiency, demonstrating how the company manages investments and financing to maintain sustainable growth. The article concludes that Apple's strong financial position, driven by innovative products and effective financial management, solidifies its leadership in the competitive technology industry.

feed.title: Apple plans to expand retail business into China
feed.summary: Apple is planning a significant expansion and revitalization of its retail chain, with 53 new, relocated, or remodeled stores globally over the next four years. The focus is heavily on the Asia-Pacific region, particularly China and India, while also upgrading locations in the US and Europe. This strategy aims to strengthen Apple's brand in growth markets and improve the customer experience.

feed.title: Miami doesn’t need to be the Silicon Valley of the South
feed.summary: Miami has been hyped as a "new Silicon Valley" or "Silicon Valley of the South," but this article argues it should be recognized for its unique strengths rather than compared to Silicon Valley. The author highlights Miami's advantages, including favorable tax laws, a supportive tech ecosystem, increasing VC attention, strong foreign investment, a focus on AI, and its role as a bridge to Latin America for nearshoring. Miami's distinct features allow it to stand as its own specialized tech hub.

feed.title: Markets in Focus: Concentrating on Diversification
feed.summary: The article discusses the increasing concentration in equity markets, exemplified by the "Magnificent Seven" stocks, and the diminished diversification benefits of bonds, especially since 2020. It suggests that investors reassess top-down allocations and broaden equity exposure across diverse regions, sectors, and styles to create more resilient portfolios. The MSCI Adaptive Multi-Factor Allocation Model indicates an overweight to value and low size as of December 31, 2023, with Europe and China trading at lower historical valuations.

feed.title: Mindlessly scroll on bike? ‘Tiktok fitness powered by Peloton’, stock soars
feed.summary: Peloton has announced a partnership with TikTok to create a new fitness hub called #TikTokFitness, powered by Peloton. This collaboration aims to bring short-form fitness videos, live classes, instructor series, and celebrity collaborations to TikTok's vast audience. Following the announcement, Peloton's stock shares rose by 7%, signaling a positive market reaction to their efforts to recover from recent financial downturns, including another recent partnership with YouTube TV.

feed.title: Google and Samsung Set To Use Qualcomm's New VR Chip, Could Compete With Apple and Meta
feed.summary: Qualcomm (QCOM) recently unveiled its new Snapdragon XR2+ Gen 2 Platform chip for virtual and augmented reality (VR/AR). Google (GOOGL) and Samsung are slated to integrate this chip into their upcoming VR/AR experiences, aiming to challenge market leaders like Apple (AAPL) and Meta Platforms (META). This development could intensify competition in the growing VR headset market, especially following Apple's Vision Pro and Meta's dominance in 2023.

feed.title: ProShares S&P 500 High Income ETF
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive profile of the ProShares S&P 500 High Income ETF (ISPY), detailing its price, dividend information, expense ratio, holdings, and performance. It includes key financial metrics, comparisons to peers, and technical analysis data. The report also highlights recent news and related ETFs within the Large Cap Blend Equities category.

feed.title: FNGU ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns AMEX:FNGU
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the MicroSectors FANG+ 3X Leveraged ETNs (FNGU), detailing its key statistics such as Assets Under Management ($1.47 billion), expense ratio (2.60%), and the fact that it does not pay dividends. It highlights FNGU's structure as a geared product with daily resets, making it suitable for short-term trading rather than long-term investment due to compounding effects. The ETF offers 3x exposure to an index of FANG companies and other similar businesses, with constituents being equally weighted.

feed.title: Aeva to supply sensors to Daimler Truck in $1 billion deal
feed.summary: Aeva Technologies has secured a $1 billion deal to supply lidar sensors to Daimler Truck for self-driving trucks, which are anticipated to be on U.S. roads by 2027. Aeva's sensors are designed to detect the speed of distant objects, aiding in collision avoidance. The agreement underscores continued interest in robotic trucking for long-haul routes despite challenges faced by self-driving passenger vehicle technology.

feed.title: FTI Consulting adds trio to Strategic Communications team
feed.summary: FTI Consulting has expanded its Strategic Communications practice in India by hiring Salonie Chawla as a Director, Snehil Singh as a Senior Consultant, and Shruti Vakhariya as a Consultant. These new additions bring diverse experience from government engagement, public policy, and financial communications to strengthen the team's capabilities in handling complex policy challenges and industry-side advisory for clients. The firm anticipates increased demand for such expertise given the disruptive business landscape expected in the coming 24 months.

feed.title: Best Socially Responsible Investment Apps of January 2024
feed.summary: This article highlights the best socially responsible investment (SRI) apps for January 2024, detailing their features, pros, and cons. It explains what SRI is, differentiates it from ESG investing, and provides expert insights on the advantages and disadvantages of using these apps. The article reviews top platforms like IMPACT, Betterment, Sustainfolio, and Ellevest, among others, to help investors align their financial goals with their ethical values.

feed.title: Software Glitch Behind Recalled App Could Drastically Increase Insulin Delivery
feed.summary: A software glitch in a recalled app has the potential to drastically increase insulin delivery, posing a significant health risk. This issue was highlighted on January 10, 2024, by Brian Bossetta.

feed.title: SKYX’s All-in-One Smart Platform is Everything from a Smart Speaker to a Smoke Detector
feed.summary: SKYX Platforms Corp. debuted its patented All-in-One Smart Platform at CES 2024, aiming to make existing homes technologically advanced. This platform integrates multiple smart home features like a smoke/CO detector, smart speaker, Wi-Fi repeater, and various lighting controls, all controllable via app and voice commands. The product installs easily into a ceiling and supports major smart home ecosystems, with SKYX also renewing a global licensing agreement with GE.

feed.title: Iridium Is Offering Its Own Smartphone-to-Satellite Service to Companies
feed.summary: Iridium has launched its own smartphone-to-satellite service, "Project Stardust," after its partnership with Qualcomm ended. This new service will allow original equipment manufacturers and carriers to offer text messaging and emergency SOS capabilities through Iridium's global low Earth orbit satellite constellation, adhering to 3GPP 5G standards. Initially focusing on data messaging, Iridium plans to begin testing in 2025 with service expected to start in 2026.

feed.title: Bracken Darrell reimagined some of the world's biggest brands. Now he has plans to do it again
feed.summary: Bracken Darrell, the interim CEO of Vans and former CEO of Logitech, is known for his ability to reinvent brands through design. Now six months into his role at VF Corp (owner of brands like Vans, North Face, and Supreme), he aims to apply his turnaround expertise to the company. Darrell previously revitalized Logitech's product line and culture, emphasizing the importance of design in corporate success.

feed.title: Upcoming Events
feed.summary: REX Shares, in collaboration with Tuttle Capital Management, has expanded its T-REX Single-Stock ETF suite with new offerings providing 200% daily targeted exposure to Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft. These ETFs (AAPX, GOOX, MSFX) are designed for seasoned traders to give leveraged exposure to major tech stocks. The T-REX suite, which debuted in October 2023, now covers diverse tech sectors and has already attracted over $100 million in assets.

feed.title: REX Shares and Tuttle Capital Management Expand T-REX Single-Stock ETF Suite with Apple, Alphabet, and Microsoft Offerings
feed.summary: REX Shares and Tuttle Capital Management have expanded their T-REX Single-Stock ETF suite by introducing new offerings for Apple (CBOE: AAPX), Alphabet (CBOE: GOOX), and Microsoft (CBOE: MSFX). These ETFs provide seasoned traders with 200% daily targeted exposure to these major tech companies. The expansion aims to offer advanced tools for precise positioning in the tech giants, building on the success of earlier NVIDIA and Tesla single-stock ETFs which have attracted over $100 million in assets since their October 2023 launch.

feed.title: Taiwan's strength in semiconductors could be its 'Achilles heel,' economist says
feed.summary: An economist warns that Taiwan's heavy reliance on semiconductor exports, particularly to China, could become its "Achilles heel." Paul Cavey of East Asia Econ advises Taiwan to diversify its economy, as China's growing capacity for chip production could significantly impact Taiwan's economic future. This vulnerability is further exacerbated by the ongoing U.S.-China chip war and efforts by Western countries to restrict China's access to advanced chip technology.

feed.title: Optimizing Communication and Data Sharing in Multi-Core SoC Designs
feed.summary: This article discusses the evolution of interconnects in Systems-on-Chip (SoCs), from shared buses to Network-on-Chip (NoC) architectures, highlighting their role in managing data flow and enhancing performance. It delves into the importance of cache coherence for data consistency in multi-core designs and the challenges engineers face in optimizing communication and data sharing as semiconductor technology advances. The article also mentions Arteris' Ncore Cache Coherent Interconnect as a solution.

feed.title: Jabil announces collaboration with Fintech Leader Revolut
feed.summary: Jabil Inc. announced its ongoing collaboration with Revolut, a digital banking pioneer, to support the neobank's growth and global expansion in merchant acquiring. Jabil provides custom, pre-certified Point-of-Sale (POS) hardware platforms, including the Revolut Reader, which enables merchants to accept various in-store and on-the-go payments. The partnership highlights Jabil's role in accelerating market entry and providing comprehensive product design, development, and manufacturing solutions for Revolut's innovative payment solutions.

feed.title: Microsoft Monetizing Copilot, Round 2; Synopsys Pays Big for Ansys
feed.summary: Microsoft is expanding the monetization of its AI-powered Copilot by removing the minimum seat requirement for Microsoft 365 Copilot subscriptions, allowing single users to purchase it. Simultaneously, Synopsys has agreed to acquire Ansys in a significant $35 billion cash and stock deal, marking a major merger in the chip design and engineering simulation software industries for 2024.

feed.title: Unlocking The Potential Of Pure Research At InterDigital
feed.summary: This article examines InterDigital's role as a pure research facility, led by CEO Liren Chen, and its significant contributions to wireless technology, including current work on 5G advanced and future plans for 6G with AI integration. It highlights InterDigital's impressive growth, long-standing partnerships with companies like Apple and Amazon, and its unique approach to fostering innovation among its largely inventor-based workforce. Chen emphasizes the importance of curiosity, competency, and impact in driving both individual and company success, showcasing InterDigital's continued influence on global digital communication.

feed.title: IDC: Apple sold more smartphones than Samsung for the first time in 2023
feed.summary: Apple Inc. surpassed Samsung Electronics Co. Ltd. to become the world's largest smartphone manufacturer by unit shipments in 2023, according to a preliminary report by IDC. This marks the first time since 2010 that Samsung did not hold the top position. Despite an overall decline in global smartphone shipments for the year, Apple saw an increase in iPhone sales, particularly strong in the fourth quarter, whereas Samsung experienced a decline.

feed.title: Supreme Court rejects Apple's appeal on App Store fees amid Epic Games legal battle
feed.summary: The Supreme Court has rejected Apple's appeal regarding its App Store fees, allowing a lower court order to take effect. This decision could force Apple to permit app developers to offer alternative payment options, potentially reducing Apple's commission revenue. The ruling stems from an antitrust lawsuit filed by Epic Games, although Epic's broader antitrust claims against Apple were also rejected.

feed.title: Disney+ On Apple Vision Pro Ushers In A New Era Of Storytelling Innovation And Immersive Entertainment
feed.summary: Beginning February 2, Disney+ will be available on Apple Vision Pro, offering subscribers a new immersive streaming experience. Users can transform their spaces into iconic Disney environments and watch dozens of popular movies in 3D, leveraging advanced technology developed through the collaboration of Disney and Apple teams. This collaboration aims to bring fans closer to beloved stories and characters through groundbreaking technology.

feed.title: Apple Inc. stock outperforms market despite losses on the day
feed.summary: Apple Inc.'s stock experienced a 0.52% decline, closing at $182.68 on Wednesday, marking its second consecutive day of losses. This occurred during an overall negative trading session where the NASDAQ Composite fell by 0.59% and the Dow Jones Industrial Average decreased by 0.25%. Despite the daily loss, the article title suggests Apple's stock performance still outpaced the broader market.

feed.title: What Does 2024 Hold for the Magnificent Seven?
feed.summary: The "Magnificent Seven" stocks, including Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta, Microsoft, Nvidia, and Tesla, drove 73% of stock market gains in 2023 due to superior earnings growth and profit margins. While some analysts predict a pullback in 2024 due to potential overvaluation and cyclicality, others believe their strong forecasted earnings growth could lead to continued outperformance, especially while interest rates remain elevated. The article highlights contrasting fortunes early in 2024, with Nvidia surging and Tesla facing its worst January since its IPO.

feed.title: 5 key themes from the 2024 Consumer Electronics Show
feed.summary: The 2024 Consumer Electronics Show (CES) highlighted five key technological themes: AI as a pervasive enabler across all tech, advancements in smart home devices with unified communication standards like Matter, the evolution of immersive technology anticipating the Apple Vision Pro, the future of the automotive industry focusing on driver experience and electric vehicles, and the widening scope of health technology, including AI-powered mental wellness tools and wearables. These innovations provide a glimpse into the technological future.

feed.title: Your S&P 500 Index Fund Has Higher Risk Than You May Think
feed.summary: This article argues that the S&P 500 index fund, traditionally seen as diversified, now carries higher risk due to its concentration in a few technology stocks. It highlights concentration, valuation, and inflation risks, suggesting investors re-evaluate their S&P 500 holdings and consider more value-oriented, truly diversified investments.

feed.title: More Computer Magazine Lore: A 1983 Nastygram from Wayne Green
feed.summary: This article provides additional lore surrounding computer magazines, focusing on Wayne Green, a colorful and controversial figure in tech publishing. It details his eccentric career, his contentious relationship with figures like Steve Jobs and David Bunnell, and his impact on early computer magazines like Byte, PC World, and Macworld. The post highlights a "nastygram" Green sent in response to PC World's premiere issue, shedding light on the competitive landscape of the 1980s computer magazine industry.

feed.title: Higher S&P 500 Earnings Could Benefit This Active ETF
feed.summary: The NEOS S&P 500 High Income ETF (SPYI) is poised to benefit from expected higher corporate earnings in 2024, as forecasted by Goldman Sachs. SPYI, an actively managed ETF, focuses on call options and holds "Magnificent Seven" stocks, offering investors tailor-made holdings, risk mitigation, and tax-efficient monthly income. Its active management removes the need for constant supervision and provides a single solution for a core equity portfolio.

feed.title: Corning's Indian JV signs $120 mln investment deal with Tamil Nadu state
feed.summary: Corning's Indian joint venture, Bharat Innovative Glass Technologies, has signed an agreement with the Tamil Nadu state to invest 10 billion rupees ($120.4 million) to establish a new facility. This plant will manufacture front cover glass for mobile phones and phone parts, highlighting India's growing importance as a manufacturing hub amid a global shift in supply chains away from China. The investment is part of larger efforts by the Indian government to promote local manufacturing and has attracted other major electronics manufacturers and Apple suppliers.

feed.title: The Best Desktop PCs: Early 2024
feed.summary: This article provides a comprehensive guide to the best pre-built desktop PCs available in early 2024, categorizing them for different needs such as gaming, all-in-one solutions, and mini PCs. It offers detailed recommendations including specific models like the Corsair Vengeance for gaming, HP Envy 34 AIO for creative work, and Apple Mac Mini M2 for compactness. The guide also discusses considerations between building a PC and buying pre-built, and factors like value, upgradeability, and space constraints.

feed.title: ‘Find My®’ Alternative Guide
feed.summary: The article provides a guide to alternatives to Apple's "Find My" app. It highlights Life360 as a location sharing and family safety application.

feed.title: Behind Target’s ‘Winning Retail Formula’
feed.summary: Target Corp. actively expanded and renovated its stores in 2023, opening 21 new locations and remodeling over 160. The company's strategy focuses on a superior shopping experience through large-format stores, expanded assortments, enhanced fulfillment services, and strategic partnerships. Target plans further investments and new locations in 2024, continuing its growth trajectory in the retail sector.

feed.title: Apple device management software company Jamf plans to layoff 6% of employees
feed.summary: Jamf, an Apple device management software company, announced a workforce reduction plan to lay off approximately 6% of its full-time employees. This move is intended to reduce operating costs, improve operating margins, and achieve profitable growth. The company expects to incur between $6.6 and $8.2 million in charges for severance and related expenses, primarily in the first quarter of 2024.

feed.title: Companies are struggling to keep private data safe from generative AI, Cisco says
feed.summary: A new Cisco report indicates that despite corporate bans and restrictions on generative AI tools, employees are still entering private company data into them. The survey of 2,600 privacy and security professionals found that a significant number of companies have either banned or limited AI usage due to concerns about data leaks and intellectual property infringement. This highlights a struggle for companies to balance the benefits of AI with the imperative to protect sensitive information.

feed.title: 10 Best Project Management Software for Windows
feed.summary: This article reviews the 10 best project management software solutions compatible with Windows, comparing their features, pricing, and specific strengths suitable for different team needs. It highlights tools like monday.com for customized workflows, Smartsheet for collaboration, Microsoft Project for scheduling, and Jira for software development, providing guidance on how to choose the right software based on budget, team size, and essential features. The article also addresses common FAQs regarding Windows project management tools, including Microsoft Project's capabilities and its transition to the upgraded Microsoft Planner.

feed.title: Newegg Debuts Newegg Refreshed Program to Offer Quality Resale Products at Low Prices
feed.summary: Newegg has launched "Newegg Refreshed," a new program offering refurbished tech products at competitive prices, including Apple goods, graphics cards, and gaming PCs. The program also integrates Newegg's graphics card trade-in service and provides a 90-day return guarantee. Newegg emphasizes both cost savings for customers and environmental benefits through product reuse.

feed.title: AI & The Dragon: What Trends Will Dominate 2024?
feed.summary: In 2024, Emerging Markets (EM) are expected to rebound, driven by potential Fed rate cuts and a weakening US dollar, with China and Brazil presenting more attractive valuations than the "Magnificent Seven" and other EM ex-China countries. The article suggests focusing on long-term trends and strategic allocations to specific growth sectors within EM. China's "Year of the Dragon" is anticipated to bring a gradual recovery in consumer sentiment and real estate, supported by government policies and significant expected EPS growth.

feed.title: What Gentex’s Acquisition Of eSight Means For The Electronic Eyewear Market
feed.summary: Gentex Corporation has acquired Toronto-based eSight, a move that could significantly impact the electronic eyewear market for the visually impaired. This acquisition follows a partnership that led to the eSight Go, a new electronic eyewear system that is more affordable, lighter, and offers improved performance compared to its predecessor. Gentex's expertise in electro-optical products and manufacturing capabilities is expected to drive down costs and enhance the technology for sight enhancement wearables.

feed.title: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) Seeks FDA Nod for Next-Gen da Vinci 5
feed.summary: Intuitive Surgical (ISRG) has applied for FDA 510(k) clearance for its next-generation da Vinci 5 multiport surgical robot and expects approval and launch later this year. The company is actively preparing manufacturing facilities for the new system while also engaging regulators in Japan and South Korea for approval. This development comes as the company's shares lost value a year ago due to the absence of a new multiport system announcement.

feed.title: Popular Inc. reports $541M in net income in 2023, $94.6M in 4Q
feed.summary: Popular Inc. reported a net income of $541.3 million for 2023, down from $1.1 billion in 2022, with $94.6 million in the fourth quarter. These figures include a $71.4 million expense due to a special FDIC assessment. Excluding this assessment, adjusted net income for 2023 was $586.6 million, and CEO Ignacio Álvarez noted stable net interest income, loan growth, and strong client activity despite market disruptions.

feed.title: Apple Inc. stock underperforms Monday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) stock underperformed on Monday, closing down 0.36% at $191.73, despite a generally positive trading session for the broader market. This marked the fourth consecutive day of losses for Apple, contrasting with gains seen in the NASDAQ Composite Index and the Dow Jones Industrial Average.

feed.title: XLK ETF: A Better Way To Invest In Microsoft But Volatility Comes First (NYSEARCA:XLK)
feed.summary: The Technology Select Sector ETF (XLK) has appreciated over 20% since October 2023, now trading above $200. The author maintains a positive outlook, attributing potential growth to top holdings like Microsoft and other software companies benefiting from AI's move into exploitation. Despite volatility risks from Fed policies and Apple's earnings, XLK is considered undervalued compared to Microsoft and Nvidia, with a target price around $223.

feed.title: Gogoro Unveils New Flagship Smartscooter, Pulse
feed.summary: Gogoro Inc. (Nasdaq: GGR) has unveiled its new flagship Smartscooter, the Pulse, which features an aerodynamic design and a hyperdrive powertrain enabling it to reach 0-50km/h in 3.05 seconds. The Pulse integrates advanced features such as an active-matrix lighting system, a 10.25-inch panoramic HD touch display powered by Qualcomm's Snapdragon QWM2290 digital chassis, and Apple Wallet and Apple Find My integration. The Smartscooter is expected to begin shipping in Taiwan in late Q2 2024, aiming to set a new standard for high-performance electric two-wheelers.

feed.title: Apple Inc. stock underperforms Tuesday when compared to competitors
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares fell 1.92% to $188.04 on Tuesday, marking its fifth consecutive day of losses. This underperformance occurred on a mixed trading day, with the Dow Jones Industrial Average rising and the NASDAQ Composite Index falling. The article highlights Apple's stock decline relative to its competitors and broader market movements.

feed.title: Corning beats quarterly sales estimates, signals improving demand for fiber optics
feed.summary: Corning exceeded Wall Street's fourth-quarter adjusted sales estimates, driven by increasing demand for optical fiber cables used in data centers for generative AI applications. Despite a slight dip in optical communications segment sales, the company observes "green shoots" in the hyperscale data center space. Although Q4 earnings met expectations, Corning provided a conservative profit outlook for the current quarter, expecting it to be the low point of the year.

feed.title: 2027 the Target for Transition Away from 1D GTIN, Toward Digital Link 2D Barcodes
feed.summary: The packaging industry is targeting 2027 for a significant transition from the traditional 1D GTIN barcode to the more versatile Digital Link 2D barcode. This shift, driven by increasing consumer demand for information, regulatory requirements, and supply chain traceability needs, will enable a single QR code to provide different information to various stakeholders (consumers, retailers, supply chain partners). The "Sunrise 2027" program aims to prepare the necessary infrastructure, primarily software changes, for this industry-wide adoption.

feed.title: Payments firm PayPal to reduce global workforce by 9% in 2024
feed.summary: Payments firm PayPal Holdings is set to cut approximately 2,500 jobs, or 9% of its global workforce, in 2024 through layoffs and the elimination of open roles. This decision, announced by CEO Alex Chriss, aims to "right-size" the company for efficiency and profitable growth amidst competitive pressures and a need to improve margins. The move follows similar cost-cutting measures by competitor Block.

feed.title: Tile vs. AirTag™: Which Bluetooth Tracker Should You Choose?
feed.summary: This article asks the question of whether a Tile or an AirTag is the better Bluetooth tracker. However, the provided content is incomplete, only listing the title and a company name, so no further details can be extracted.

feed.title: Apple Inc. stock underperforms Thursday when compared to competitors despite daily gains
feed.summary: Apple Inc. (AAPL) shares rose 1.33% to $186.86 on Thursday, breaking a six-day losing streak. Despite this gain, the stock underperformed compared to the broader market, as the NASDAQ Composite Index and Dow Jones Industrial Average both saw larger percentage increases on the same day.

feed.title: NEOS Nasdaq 100 High Income ETF
feed.summary: This page provides comprehensive information on the NEOS Nasdaq 100 High Income ETF (QQQI), including its stock profile, dividends, expense ratio, holdings, ESG scores, and performance data as of April 3, 2026. QQQI is an actively managed large-cap blend equity ETF with an expense ratio of 0.68% and significant AUM of $9,433.8 million, showing strong fund flows over several periods. The ETF primarily invests in major technology companies and aims to provide high income, reflected in its substantial annual dividend yield of 16.04%.

feed.title: Alcoa to supply Nexans with low-carbon aluminum, including metal from ELYSIS™ technology
feed.summary: Alcoa will supply Nexans with low-carbon aluminum rod, including metal produced using the breakthrough ELYSIS™ technology, which eliminates direct greenhouse gas emissions from the traditional smelting process. Nexans will be the first cable manufacturer globally to utilize metal from this process in various cable types. This collaboration further strengthens the long-standing relationship between the two companies, with Alcoa already supplying Nexans with EcoLum™ low-carbon aluminum.

feed.title: Box Brings Your Content to Life with the Box App for Apple Vision Pro
feed.summary: Box Inc. has launched a new app for Apple Vision Pro, leveraging spatial computing to transform how users interact with content in the enterprise. The Box app for Apple Vision Pro offers immersive experiences for various industries, including construction, retail, manufacturing, and education. It provides secure collaboration and productivity features, integrating with Box's Content Cloud, and will be available for free on the App Store for visionOS on February 2, 2024.

feed.title: Aptiv to cut stake in Motional, self-driving JV with Hyundai
feed.summary: Aptiv, a US autonomous driving technology company, has decided to significantly reduce its stake in Motional, a self-driving joint venture with Hyundai Motor Group, and will cease injecting additional capital. Motional, which has accumulated 1 trillion won ($750 million) in deficits, had requested further funding from both partners. This decision puts pressure on Hyundai Motor Group to decide on its own additional capital injection, highlighting the ongoing financial and commercial challenges in the autonomous vehicle industry.

feed.title: Disney Heads into the Future on the Apple Vision Pro
feed.summary: The Walt Disney Company is launching Disney+ on Apple's Vision Pro, marking a new chapter in immersive storytelling. This integration allows viewers to experience Disney+ content in virtual environments like Star Wars' Tatooine or Marvel's Avengers Tower, and watch 3D versions of popular films. The partnership highlights Disney's long-standing focus on blending technology with creativity to enhance entertainment experiences.

feed.title: Apple, Skechers fall; Amazon, Deckers Outdoor rise, Friday, 2/2/2024
feed.summary: On Friday, February 2, 2024, several major stocks experienced significant price changes. Apple and Skechers saw declines due to lukewarm forecasts, while Amazon, Deckers Outdoor, Meta Platforms, and Clorox rose following strong financial results or optimistic outlooks. Microchip Technology and Columbia Sportswear also faced drops after giving disappointing forecasts.

feed.title: What Stock Investors Need to Know About Meta’s New Dividend
feed.summary: Meta Platforms announced its first-ever quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, alongside its fourth-quarter earnings, leading to a significant stock surge. While the dividend provides a more balanced capital return program, share repurchases will remain the primary method for returning cash to shareholders. The initial dividend yield is modest compared to peers and the broader market index, but Morningstar analysts expect it to grow.

feed.title: Markets Brief: Will the ‘Magnificent Seven’ Stocks Keep Carrying the Market After Earnings?
feed.summary: The article analyzes the performance of the "Magnificent Seven" stocks after their latest earnings reports, noting that while most beat forecasts, market reactions were mixed due to high expectations. It discusses whether these tech giants can continue to drive market gains in 2024, given their current valuations and the potential for a broader market rally. The article also provides a detailed recap of individual company performances and analyst commentary.

feed.title: Vishay Unveils New High-Speed Photodiode for Wearable Devices
feed.summary: Vishay Intertechnology has expanded its optoelectronics portfolio with the VEMD2074, a new high-speed silicon PIN photodiode designed for wearable devices. This photodiode offers improved visible light sensitivity, low capacitance, and quick switching times, making it ideal for accurate signal detection in applications like pulse oximetry and optical heart rate detection. Its small size, flexibility in sensor placement, and cost-effectiveness facilitate integration into compact and price-sensitive products such as smartwatches and earbuds.

feed.title: Inside the JOET Rebalance
feed.summary: Joe Terranova, senior managing director at Virtus Investment Partners, appeared on CNBC's 'Halftime Report' to discuss the rebalance of his JOET ETF portfolio. The report highlights his insights into the adjustments made to his investment strategy.

feed.title: These ETFs could help investors reduce Big Tech exposure
feed.summary: Investors who are overly concentrated in Big Tech stocks are looking for ways to diversify their portfolios. Equal-weight ETFs like the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight ETF and the Invesco S&P 500 Equal Weight Technology ETF are suggested as options to spread risk beyond top-heavy Magnificent Seven companies, as advisors note nervousness about Big Tech valuations.

feed.title: What investors need to know about Meta’s new dividend
feed.summary: Meta Platforms Inc. has announced its first-ever quarterly dividend of $0.50 per share, which will be paid on March 26 to shareholders of record on February 22. This move is seen as a complement to existing share repurchase programs, which will remain the primary method of returning capital to shareholders. While the market reacted positively, sending Meta's stock up 20%, the initial dividend yield of less than 0.5% is modest compared to its mega-cap peers and the Morningstar Dividend Composite Index.

feed.title: ¿Cuáles son los CEDEAR más elegidos por los argentinos?
feed.summary: Certificados de Depósito Argentinos (CEDEAR) are a popular investment option in Argentina for diversifying portfolios and hedging against peso devaluation by investing in foreign companies. In January, Coca-Cola (KO) was the most chosen CEDEAR, followed by Apple (AAPL) and Tesla (TSLA). Recent changes by Banco Comafi regarding conversion ratios aim to make CEDEARs more accessible to a wider range of investors by lowering entry prices.

feed.title: ESPN, Fox, and Warner Bros. Discovery Team Up to Offer Joint Sports Streaming Service
feed.summary: Walt Disney's ESPN, Fox, and Warner Bros. Discovery are collaborating to launch a joint sports streaming service for U.S. customers available in the fall. This new service aims to combine their sports offerings, including professional and college games, to better compete with rivals like Comcast and Apple who have seen success in sports streaming. The app will be available standalone or bundled with Disney+, Hulu, or Max, consolidating vast sports rights under one platform.

feed.title: ITOT ETF: Investing Well Doesn’t Have to Be Complicated
feed.summary: The iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF (ITOT) offers a straightforward, low-cost way to invest in a broad range of U.S. companies, providing exposure to 90% of the total domestic stock market. With an ultra-low expense ratio of 0.03% and a track record of strong double-digit annualized returns over the past decade, ITOT is presented as an effective long-term portfolio building block due to its wide diversification across 2,589 stocks. Despite its simplicity, it has delivered significant wealth building for investors since its inception in 2004.

feed.title: ITOT ETF: Investing Well Doesn’t Have to Be Complicated
feed.summary: The iShares Core S&P Total U.S. Stock Market ETF (ITOT) is presented as an effective, uncomplicated, and low-cost investment option for long-term growth. The article highlights ITOT's strong historical performance with double-digit annualized returns, wide diversification across 2,589 U.S. stocks, and an ultra-low expense ratio of 0.03%. It emphasizes the fund's lack of concentration risk due to its extensive holdings and its ability to provide exposure beyond just large-cap stocks.

feed.title: One Store attracts $50 mn investment from Digital Turbine
feed.summary: South Korean app store One Store has secured a $50 million investment from US-based mobile platform company Digital Turbine, with an initial $10 million for new shares. This partnership aims to facilitate One Store's overseas expansion and business growth, leveraging Digital Turbine's global reach and app advertising services. The investment raises One Store's corporate value to over 920 billion won ($694 million), nearing the 1 trillion won mark, and follows previous strategic investments from DTCP and Krafton Inc.

feed.title: ADT welcomes Silicon Valley veteran Gilles Drieu as new CTO
feed.summary: ADT has appointed Gilles Drieu, a veteran of Silicon Valley tech giants like Google, Apple, and Adobe, as its new Chief Technology Officer. Drieu will work alongside Chief Product Officer Naveen Chhangani and Chief Information Officer Sam Jaddi to accelerate ADT's innovation in smart home security offerings. His experience includes leading the development of HTML5-enabled web browsers at Apple and pivotal work on Chromecast, Home, and Nest products at Google.

feed.title: Apple defeats lawsuit claiming it overpaid CEO Tim Cook, others
feed.summary: A federal judge dismissed a lawsuit against Apple alleging overpayment of CEO Tim Cook and other top executives through miscalculated performance-based stock awards. The judge ruled that Apple's compensation disclosures met securities law requirements and found no proof of improper actions by the board, also noting the plaintiffs did not allow sufficient time for the board to address their objections before filing suit. The lawsuit, brought by a Teamsters-affiliated pension fund, claimed Apple overvalued restricted stock units in 2021 and 2022.

feed.title: JGLO Stock Price | Jpmorgan Global Select Equity ETF
feed.summary: This article provides detailed information on the Jpmorgan Global Select Equity ETF (JGLO), including its current stock price, trading exchange (NASDAQ), and key statistics like dividend yield, expense ratio, and holdings. It also offers technical analysis over various timeframes and lists the top companies held within the ETF, such as NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Amazon. The ETF, managed by J.P. Morgan Investment Management Inc., invests in socially conscious global developed equity markets, mirroring the MSCI World Index.

feed.title: DLN: Large-Cap Monthly Dividend Payer Not So Hot
feed.summary: The WisdomTree US LargeCap Dividend ETF (DLN) is rated a "Hold" due to its lower dividend yield and growth rate compared to other dividend-paying ETFs. While DLN offers monthly income and is well-diversified across approximately 300 companies, its current concentration in technology and large-cap focus present a limitation given the potential for future small-cap growth. The article suggests that while large-cap growth has dominated, changing market dynamics could favor value and small-cap opportunities.

feed.title: Nvidia Overtakes Amazon in Market Value
feed.summary: Nvidia briefly surpassed Amazon in market value, making it the fourth most valuable US-listed company earlier in the day. The chipmaker's rally, driven by exploding demand for AI chips, has seen its shares jump 46% this year. While Nvidia closed slightly below Amazon's valuation, the event marks a significant milestone fueled by the AI boom.

feed.title: Meta settles lawsuit over haptic feedback tech in Quest headsets
feed.summary: Immersion, a company specializing in haptic feedback technology, has settled a lawsuit with Meta over Meta's use of touch-feedback in its Quest VR headsets. The settlement involves Meta licensing Immersion's patents for its hardware, software, VR, and gaming products, though the terms were not disclosed. Immersion has a history of pursuing patent infringement lawsuits against major tech companies like Microsoft, Sony, Apple, and Valve.

feed.title: Michael Burry Covers Bet Against Chips ETF, Buys Health and Tech Stocks
feed.summary: Michael Burry's Scion Asset Management has changed its investment strategy, abandoning its bearish bet against semiconductor companies by closing its position against the iShares Semiconductor ETF (SOXX). Instead, the firm nearly doubled its holdings to 25 positions, acquiring shares in health and technology sectors, and holds no bearish positions as of the end of 2023. Burry, known from "The Big Short," had previously bet against the S&P 500 and Nasdaq 100 indexes.

feed.title: Relx Share Price Spiked Up 3% but Later Fell on FY 2023 Results
feed.summary: Relx PLC announced strong FY 2023 financial results, reporting an 8% organic revenue growth to £9,161 million and a 13% organic adjusted operating profit increase to £3,030 million. The company proposed an 8% higher full-year dividend of 58.8p per share. Despite an initial 3% spike in its share price, it later fell, trading at 3430.5p.

feed.title: YieldMax Magnificent 7 Fund of Option Income ETFs
feed.summary: This article provides a detailed profile of the YieldMax Magnificent 7 Fund of Option Income ETFs (YMAG), including its price, category, expenses, inception date, and top holdings. It also presents analysis on its performance, fund flows, AUM influence, and ESG scores, comparing it against ETF Database category averages and FactSet segment averages. The fund's primary holdings are option income strategy ETFs for individual "Magnificent 7" stocks.

feed.title: Stock market concentration is on the rise. Will this continue?
feed.summary: Stock market concentration in the U.S. has reached multi-decade highs, largely driven by the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks. While these mega-caps have benefited from market rotation and AI excitement, historical trends suggest that steep rises in concentration tend to reverse, and this could be influenced by future liquidity and the economic cycle. Investors should consider the implications of this concentration on future performance, especially in different economic scenarios like a soft landing.

feed.title: Can Korea fend off China in display race?
feed.summary: China is rapidly gaining on Korea in the display market, particularly in OLED technology for mobile phones, driven by state subsidies and technology transfers. While China has already surpassed Korea in LCDs, Korea is focusing on IT OLEDs and innovative TV features to maintain its competitive edge. Despite China's increasing market share in terms of volume, Korea still leads in sales value for premium displays.

feed.title: Stryker updates Mako surgical robot, introduces joint replacement offering
feed.summary: Stryker announced new advancements for its Mako surgical robotic platform, including the myMako app for Apple Vision Pro and iPhone and the Direct Anterior Reconstructive Technology (DART). The company also introduced Triathlon Hinge, a new offering in its Triathlon brand, designed to simplify revision procedures for complex cases involving bone loss or soft-tissue challenges. These updates were showcased at AAOS in San Francisco, with further Mako expansions, including Mako Spine and Mako Shoulder, anticipated later in 2024.

feed.title: Here's How Much Money You'd Have if You Invested $7,000 in the "Magnificent Seven" in 2017
feed.summary: This article examines the investment returns if one had invested $7,000 across the "Magnificent Seven" stocks (Alphabet, Amazon, Apple, Meta Platforms, Microsoft, Nvidia, Tesla) in 2017. It highlights the significant growth these companies experienced, with Nvidia and Tesla showing the highest returns, and compares this performance to an investment in the S&P 500 over the same period. The author concludes that these established growth stocks can still offer good long-term returns despite their current size.

feed.title: US Supreme Court won't review Apple's win against $503 million patent verdict
feed.summary: The U.S. Supreme Court has declined to hear a patent-licensing company's bid to revive a $502.8 million jury verdict against Apple. VirnetX had sued Apple over internet-security patents, but the Patent and Trademark Office later invalidated those patents. This decision upholds a lower court's ruling which overturned the initial verdict.

feed.title: Price-conscious shoppers lift Walmart earnings as it preps $2.3 billion Vizio deal
authors: Siddharth Cavale and Ananya Mariam Rajesh
feed.summary: Walmart reported robust fourth-quarter results, driven by inflation-squeezed shoppers seeking value, performing better than rivals in a broad slowdown of discretionary spending. The company also announced a $2.3 billion acquisition of smart-TV maker Vizio, aiming to bolster its advertising business, Walmart Connect, and enhance its presence in consumers' homes. This strategic move aligns with Walmart's plan to diversify its profit streams, with a greater focus on advertising and online marketplace businesses in the coming years.

feed.title: The Companies We’re Watching During Proxy-Voting Season 2024
feed.summary: The 2024 proxy-voting season is marked by increased shareholder activism, particularly focusing on environmental, social, and governance (ESG) issues like climate impacts, workers' rights, and AI oversight. Key companies like Tesla, Apple, and Applied Materials are under scrutiny for their board governance, compensation, and ethical AI guidelines, amid growing regulatory momentum for climate disclosures. The season also highlights new opportunities for individual investors and increased scrutiny of "anti-ESG" resolutions.

feed.title: Dimensional ETFs and More: New on Syfe Custom Portfolio
feed.summary: Syfe Custom Portfolio is expanding its offerings with new equity ETFs from Dimensional Fund Advisors, focusing on value and quality tilts, alongside additional quality-focused and equally-weighted Nasdaq ETFs. The article details Dimensional's factor-based investing approach and highlights specific ETFs added to the platform, including options for accredited investors such as Bitcoin ETFs and a Nasdaq Equity Premium Income ETF. Syfe also explains how to utilize Custom Portfolios to complement existing investments by allowing tactical allocation based on asset class, risk, geography, and sector.

feed.title: Episode #522: Wes Gray & Robert Elwood on How to Convert a Separately Managed Account (SMA) to an ETF
feed.summary: This podcast episode features Wes Gray and Robert Elwood discussing their experience in converting Separately Managed Accounts (SMAs) to Exchange Traded Funds (ETFs) via Section 351, including a recent conversion of $770 million. They delve into the process, advantages, and challenges of such conversions, highlighting tax efficiencies and the broader implications for financial advisors and family offices. The discussion also touches upon the future of asset management and the rising trend of SMA to ETF conversions.

feed.title: How Nvidia grew to a trillion-dollar company fueling the AI revolution
feed.summary: Nvidia has grown into a nearly $3 trillion company by specializing in Graphics Processing Units (GPUs), which have become crucial for the artificial intelligence revolution. Initially used for gaming, GPUs' ability to perform concurrent calculations makes them ideal for AI, giving Nvidia a significant first-mover advantage and market dominance. The company's unique technology and supply chain have led to widespread adoption by major tech firms, with discussions emerging about potential shortages and the need for continued investment in chip manufacturing.

feed.title: FFLG Top Holdings List & Exposure (Fidelity Fundamental Large Cap Growth ETF)
feed.summary: This article details the top holdings and exposure of the Fidelity Fundamental Large Cap Growth ETF (FFLG). It lists the top 25 stocks held by FFLG, including NVIDIA, Microsoft, and Apple, which collectively represent 77.72% of the fund. The report also breaks down FFLG's geographic, currency, sector, and industry exposures, showing a strong focus on the United States and the Technology sector.

feed.title: Apple Kills Its Electric Car Project (Published 2024)
feed.summary: Apple has canceled its decade-long project to develop an electric, self-driving car, a move announced internally to employees. This decision shifts many of the car project's staff to Apple's artificial intelligence division, marking a rare public setback for the company. The car, codenamed Titan, was intended to rival Tesla's E.V.s and bolster Apple's innovation legacy, but faced development challenges and financial investment of billions of dollars.

feed.title: Moomoo Launches All-in-One Investment Super App in Malaysia; Empowering Local Financial Digitalisation
feed.summary: Moomoo has launched its all-in-one investment super app in Malaysia, aiming to promote financial inclusion and digitalize the local capital market. The company, which is among the first digital brokerage firms to receive a Capital Market Services License from the Securities Commission Malaysia, offers a professional and innovative digital investment experience. Moomoo provides access to a wide range of stocks and ETFs, AI-powered tools, educational resources, and a global investment community.

feed.title: NBA® 2K24: MyTEAM Available Worldwide on iOS and Android
feed.summary: NBA® 2K24: MyTEAM, a free-to-download mobile game, is now available worldwide on iOS and Android, allowing players to synchronize their progress between console and mobile devices. This enables users to assemble their dream NBA lineups, play various MyTEAM modes, earn XP, and collect rewards on the go. The game features quality NBA 2K gameplay, stunning graphics, and compatibility with external controllers, offering a realistic NBA simulation experience.

feed.title: A Wonderful Options-Based Dividend-Focused ETF?
feed.summary: This article discusses the new Swan Global Enhanced Dividend Income Strategy (SCLZ) ETF, which combines Swan Global's options expertise with O'Shares Investment's stock selection. The ETF aims to provide sustainable retirement income through active management of options on dividend-growing companies, focusing on profitability and quality stocks. Key takeaways from a recent webcast highlighted the importance of active management, a profitability-based stock index, and a preference for "boring companies" that consistently generate dividends.

feed.title: NANC ETF Tracking Democrat Stock Trades Beating S&P 500 on Tech Bets
feed.summary: The Unusual Whales Democratic ETF (NANC), which tracks stock trades by Democrats in Congress, has outperformed the S&P 500 since its launch in February 2023, returning 30% compared to the S&P 500's 24%. This outperformance is largely attributed to its significant holdings in mega-cap tech stocks like Microsoft, Amazon, Apple, and Nvidia. In contrast, a similar ETF tracking Republican trades (KRUZ) has only gained about 15% over the same period.

feed.title: EU lawmakers back draft rules on patents for connected cars, telecom equipment
feed.summary: EU lawmakers have approved draft rules concerning patents for technologies in connected cars and telecom equipment, despite criticism from patent holders like Nokia and Ericsson. The proposed rules aim to resolve expensive and prolonged patent disputes, while industry groups like IP Europe argue they could stifle innovation, whereas the Fair Standards Alliance supports them for promoting fair licensing terms. The rules will now proceed to negotiations between the European Parliament and EU member countries.

feed.title: VONG ETF: A Great Way to Invest in Top Growth Stocks
feed.summary: The Vanguard Russell 1000 Growth ETF (VONG) offers a straightforward and effective way to invest in a diverse range of top growth stocks. With a strong track record of impressive returns, a low expense ratio of 0.08%, and a portfolio that includes leading mega-cap technology companies and other high-growth firms, VONG is considered a compelling option for investors seeking exposure to large-cap growth in the U.S. market. The ETF's holdings are highly rated, with many featuring Outperform-equivalent Smart Scores.

feed.title: Nvidia skyrocketed over the past 5 years, but 3 stocks performed even better—and none is Apple or Tesla
feed.summary: While Nvidia has achieved an impressive 1,904% return over the past five years, three mid-cap stocks have significantly outpaced it: e.l.f. Beauty (2,491%), Super Micro Computer (4,175%), and Celsius Holdings (over 5,300%). The article highlights that smaller companies (mid-caps) often show greater growth potential and less media coverage than large-cap stocks like Nvidia, suggesting diversification into these smaller companies can boost long-term returns. Investors are advised to use diversified funds (ETFs and mutual funds) to mitigate volatility and risk associated with individual small-cap stock picks.

feed.title: Butterfly iQ+ Ultrasound System
feed.summary: Butterfly iQ+ is a second-generation handheld, single-probe ultrasound system developed by Butterfly Network, utilizing Ultrasound-on-Chip™ semiconductor technology for diagnostic imaging. It has received regulatory approvals in the US and Canada and offers enhanced features for various medical applications, with subsequent generations (iQ3) further improving performance and imaging capabilities. Partnerships with Sientra and Abdul Latif Jameel Health aim to expand its distribution and application.

feed.title: Dow's nearly 125-point rally led by gains for shares of NVIDIA Corp., Cisco
feed.summary: The Dow Jones Industrial Average rose by nearly 125 points, primarily driven by strong performances from NVIDIA Corp. and Cisco Systems Inc. NVIDIA's shares surged after an analyst upgrade, while Cisco remained relatively flat despite an analyst reiteration of an overweight rating. The overall market saw NVIDIA and Cisco among the top gainers, contrasting with losses from other Dow components.

feed.title: Magnificent Seven vs. the ‘Granolas’: How does Europe’s version stack up?
feed.summary: This article compares the U.S. "Magnificent Seven" stocks with Europe's "Granolas," highlighting their differences in market value, sector diversity, and profitability. While the Magnificent Seven are largely tech-driven and exceptionally profitable, the Granolas offer broader sector representation and solid returns, often trading at a discount despite their strong performance. The piece examines historical returns, valuation ratios, and individual stock assessments for both groups.

feed.title: Apple Hit With €1.8 Billion EU Fine Over Music App Abuses
feed.summary: The European Union has fined Apple Inc. €1.8 billion ($2 billion) for allegedly stifling competition from music-streaming rivals like Spotify. The EU also mandated that Apple stop preventing apps from informing users about cheaper subscription options outside its App Store. This fine marks the first time the EU has penalized Apple directly.

feed.title: Eye on Travel: AmEx’s New Virtual Card Integration for Small-Business Travel and Expenses; IHG Hotels Endorses Grazzy for Digital Tipping
feed.summary: American Express has launched a new virtual card integration with AmEx Global Business Travel's Neo1 platform, aiming to simplify travel and expense management for U.S. small and mid-size businesses using AmEx business and corporate cards. Separately, Grazzy Inc. has been named an approved partner by IHG Hotels and Resorts to provide digital tipping solutions for hotel employees. Grazzy's platform allows guests to tip staff electronically without a dedicated app, offering various payment methods and providing hotels with reporting capabilities for tip income and program performance.

feed.title: ‘Funflation’ & Technology Driving Music Industry Resurgence
feed.summary: The music industry is experiencing a resurgence fueled by "funflation" (increased spending on live events) and technological advancements such as streaming, AI, and blockchain. Record-breaking attendance at concerts, rising streaming subscriptions, and innovation are driving growth for companies like Live Nation, Universal Music, and Warner Music Group. The MUSQ ETF offers a pure-play investment into this evolving and increasingly monetized global music market.

feed.title: HP Inc. CEO: Security Will Be A ‘Key Advantage’ In Our AI PC Strategy
feed.summary: HP CEO Enrique Lores announced that security will be a "key advantage" in the company's AI PC strategy, emphasizing local large language model processing for enhanced security, lower costs, and faster performance compared to cloud solutions. HP plans to leverage its Wolf Security technologies to protect AI models running on these new PCs, which are expected to launch in the second half of 2024. Lores believes AI PCs represent a significant opportunity for channel partners due to device sales and the coming wave of AI-optimized software.

feed.title: How to Invest in Technology
feed.summary: This article provides an overview of how to invest in the technology sector, highlighting its rapid evolution and significant growth over the past two decades. It breaks down key tech market developments, discusses sectors with high potential like AI, mobile gaming, 3D printing, and blockchain, and offers various investment methods including ETFs and individual stocks.

feed.title: Why Mega-Cap Tech Stocks’ Dominance Is a Risk
feed.summary: The "Magnificent 7" tech stocks now comprise a significant 30% of the S&P 500 Index’s market capitalization, creating concentration risks for investors. This historical top-heaviness makes portfolios vulnerable to significant drops if these stocks, highly correlated and sensitive to interest rates, decline. The article suggests strategies like investing in U.S. bonds, non-U.S. equities, equal-weighted S&P 500 strategies, and alternative assets to mitigate this risk.

feed.title: Should iShares Russell 1000 ETF (IWB) Be on Your Investing Radar?
feed.summary: The iShares Russell 1000 ETF (IWB) offers broad exposure to the Large Cap Blend segment of the US equity market, with over $34.55 billion in assets and a low expense ratio of 0.15%. Featuring top holdings like Microsoft, Apple, and Nvidia, the ETF has performed well, returning 7.02% year-to-date and 27.20% over the last year. It holds a Zacks ETF Rank of 3 (Hold) and is considered a good option for investors seeking exposure to large-cap blend stocks, with alternatives like IVV and SPY also available.

feed.title: WhatsApp to Allow Cross-Functional Chatting Soon
feed.summary: WhatsApp is preparing to introduce cross-platform interoperability, allowing its users to chat with contacts on other messaging apps without leaving WhatsApp. This significant policy shift is influenced by the EU’s Digital Markets Act, which designated Meta as a "gatekeeper" and requires the company to open its messaging services. The feature, initially limited to one-on-one messaging, aims to enhance user convenience but presents complex security challenges due to differing encryption standards across platforms.

feed.title: SCHX: As Cheap As It Gets For Large-Cap Exposure
feed.summary: This article recommends the Schwab U.S. Large-Cap ETF (SCHX) for investors seeking exposure to large-cap stocks, highlighting its diversification, low costs, and tax efficiency. Key holdings include tech giants like Microsoft and Apple, leading to significant exposure to the Information Technology sector. The author, Michael A. Gayed, CFA, discusses market efficiency and offers insights from his Lead-Lag Report service.

feed.title: Fortune 500 list: The top 10 companies dominating business
feed.summary: This article details the top 10 companies on the 2023 Fortune 500 list, ranked by revenue, ahead of the 2024 list release. Walmart maintains the top spot for the eleventh consecutive year, followed by Amazon, Exxon Mobil, and Apple. The report provides key financial figures, strategic moves, and recent developments for each company, highlighting their influence on the global economy.

feed.title: TSMC to Win More Than $5 Billion in Grants for US Chip Plant
feed.summary: Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Co. (TSMC) is expected to receive over $5 billion in federal grants for its chipmaking project in Arizona, a significant move for President Biden's bid to revitalize American semiconductor manufacturing. This award, part of the 2022 Chips and Science Act, is not yet finalized but marks a major step in distributing funds to leading chipmakers like Intel, Micron Technology, and Samsung. TSMC is investing $40 billion in two Arizona fabrication facilities, though the project has faced delays due to labor shortages and technological considerations.

feed.title: Alphabet is the bargain stock among the ‘Magnificent Seven’
feed.summary: Alphabet, Google's holding company, is highlighted as a compelling investment opportunity among the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks due to its valuation. Its stock trades at a lower price/earnings valuation than the S&P 500, positioning it as a bargain compared to its peers and the broader market. This situation is likened to the opportunity presented by Meta's stock after its 2022 decline.

feed.title: COPP Stock Price | Sprott Copper Miners ETF
authors: Luis Flavio Nunes (for an embedded news article) or NULL
feed.summary: This article provides an in-depth look at the Sprott Copper Miners ETF (COPP), including its current stock price, trading exchange, and key financial statistics like expense ratio, dividends, and asset composition. It also features a company profile, performance data, and holdings information, alongside technical analysis and recent news related to copper prices. The ETF invests in public equity markets within the materials, metals, mining, and copper sectors.

feed.title: Apple v. Rivos: Lessons for Companies Facing Claims of Trade Secret Theft (US)
authors: Karen Wentzel and Squire Patton Boggs
feed.summary: This article discusses the lessons learned from the Apple v. Rivos case regarding trade secret theft. It highlights that mere possession of trade secrets is insufficient for misappropriation claims against a new employer and emphasizes the importance of explicit policies against bringing confidential information from former employers. The ruling provides key takeaways for companies to guide their trade secret policies and practices to avoid litigation.

feed.title: 3 Stocks to Buy Before the Fintech Sector Starts to Sizzle Again
feed.summary: The fintech sector, though currently lukewarm compared to other tech areas, is poised for a rebound as the economic landscape improves. SoFi Technologies (SOFI) is navigating a capital raise but remains a key beneficiary of potential Fed rate cuts, while Block (SQ) is showing signs of recovery, boosted by renewed consumer spending and crypto enthusiasm. Apple (AAPL), despite its focus on AI and new devices, has a significant and growing fintech services business that could drive future earnings.

feed.title: Inverse/Leveraged Magnificent Seven ETFs Hit the Market
feed.summary: Roundhill Investments has launched two new ETFs focusing on the "Magnificent Seven" tech stocks: the Roundhill Daily 2X Long Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGX) and the Roundhill Daily Inverse Magnificent Seven ETF (MAGQ). MAGX offers 2x leveraged daily exposure to the Magnificent Seven, while MAGQ provides inverse (-1X) daily exposure. These funds cater to investors looking to capitalize on both potential gains and losses of these influential tech giants, which significantly impact the S&P 500 and are highly valued, indicating a need for both bullish and bearish investment options.

feed.title: The Apple Effect: Magic Leap Founder’s Previous Company Launches App on Vision Pro
